journal_entry.description: Purchased merchandise on account with freight-in, insurance, and related costs
journal_entry.rationale: Merchandise inventory increases by $1,150,000 (purchase price $1,000,000 + freight-in $120,000 + shipping insurance $30,000). These costs are necessary to bring inventory to its sellable condition and location, so they are capitalized to inventory. Post-acquisition warehouse storage ($20,000) and outbound delivery freight ($50,000) are period costs incurred after acquisition, expensed as incurred. Accounts payable increases for the merchandise purchased on account, and cash decreases for the four cash payments totaling $220,000.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Merchandise purchase with mixed costs: freight-in and insurance capitalize to inventory (inbound costs necessary to get goods ready for sale); post-acquisition storage and outbound delivery are operating expenses. Two asset increases (inventory + expenses), one liability increase, one asset decrease (cash).
